prose:
Frontier-orbital energies of pyridine computed at the DFT level using the B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral closed-shell singlet, RKS). Geometry taken from the user-supplied XYZ file as-is (single-point, not re-optimized at DFT level). No density fitting (RI) was used — exact four-center integrals. SCF converged in 10 cycles. Integrity status: trustworthy=true, all checks passed. No warnings reported (clean run).

HOMO (index 20): −7.2525992 eV
LUMO (index 21): −1.1127860 eV
HOMO–LUMO gap (headline value): 6.1398131 eV (Koopmans-style orbital gap, not an excitation energy)

Additional orbitals:
  HOMO−2 (index 18): −8.0425195 eV
  HOMO−1 (index 19): −7.4731156 eV
  LUMO+1 (index 22): −0.6723154 eV
  LUMO+2 (index 23): +1.1781069 eV

Koopmans descriptors: vertical IP = 7.2525992 eV, vertical EA = 1.1127860 eV, electronegativity = 4.1826926 eV, chemical hardness = 3.0699066 eV, softness = 0.32574281 eV⁻¹, electrophilicity = 2.8494218 eV.

Total electronic energy: −248.3782 Hartree (−6758.7152 eV). 216 total orbitals, 21 occupied.

CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier pyridine.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1. Output JSON: pyridine_frontier_dft_b3lyp_def2tzvp.json.
